code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test11-OCT-2017Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows mild hypoglycemia and a low HbA1c, suggesting possible chronic low glucose. Elevated creatinine indicates impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, ketonuria, bilirubinuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance, suggesting possible nephrotic syndrome or acute kidney injury. No evidence of urinary infection. Recommend further renal evaluation and assessment of glycemic status.

coding.display: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus

Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
